title: thymelaea granatensis subsp. glauca
intro: Thymelaea granatensis subsp. glauca, also known as the Tree Madder or Spurge Laurel, is a perennial shrub belonging to the Thymelaeaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, including glaucine and berberine. The health benefits of Thymelaea granatensis subsp. glauca include its potential to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, as well as its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and respiratory problems.
